---
title: "max_pool_with_indices_nd"
generated: true
description: "N-D max pool returning `(values, indices)`."
---

[ΓÇ╣ back to `nitrix.morphology.pooling`](../nitrix-morphology-pooling.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:max_pool_with_indices_nd -->
```python
max_pool_with_indices_nd(x: Num[Array, '*batch C *spatial'], *, pool_size: PoolSize, spatial_rank: int) -> Tuple[Num[Array, '*batch C *pooled'], Int[Array, '*batch C *pooled']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:max_pool_with_indices_nd -->
N-D max pool returning `(values, indices)`.

For each output position, returns the max value in its
corresponding window (per-channel) and the flattened index
of the argmax within the original spatial grid (per-channel,
per-output).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:max_pool_with_indices_nd -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Num` | `*batch` ├ù `C` ├ù `*spatial` | ΓÇË | Channel-first input, `(..., C, *spatial)`. Leading `...` axes are batch. |
| `pool_size` | `PoolSize` | ΓÇË | ΓÇË | Window size per spatial axis. `int` = isotropic; `tuple` of length `spatial_rank` for per-axis. Spatial dimensions must be divisible by `pool_size` (no padding logic; caller pads upstream if needed). |
| `spatial_rank` | `int` | ΓÇË | ΓÇË | Number of trailing spatial axes; explicit to disambiguate batch vs channel. |

**Returns** `Tuple[Num[Array, '*batch C *pooled'], Int[Array, '*batch C *pooled']]`. divided by `pool_size`. Per-channel max within each window. flattened C-order index (into the *unbatched, single-channel* spatial grid) of the argmax voxel for that output position. [`max_unpool_nd`](../nitrix-morphology-pooling/max_unpool_nd.qmd) consumes this directly.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:max_pool_with_indices_nd -->
::: {.callout-note}
Implementation: reshape the spatial axes via "unfold" so each
window becomes its own axis, then `jnp.argmax` along the
unfolded axes (flattened) gives the within-window index.
Combined with the window's starting voxel coordinates, this
yields the global flat index.  Cost: `O(N)` in voxels with
`O(N)` HBM (no per-window scan).

Ties: when multiple voxels share the window max, returns the
first argmax (lowest flat index within the window) -- matches
`jnp.argmax` behaviour.  Cross-framework consumers should
expect tie-breaking to differ; see the module docstring on
parity.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:max_pool_with_indices_nd -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*batch` | leading batch axes (variadic) |
| `C` | *(undocumented ΓÇË add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
